commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And they shall make an ark of acacia wood: two cubits and a half shall be the length of it, and a cubit and a half the breadth of it, and a cubit and a half the height of it. Ark, to contain the tables of the law, as a constant memorial of the alliance made between God and his people, ver. In, or on the side of it, were also placed the rod of Aaron, (Numbers xvii. ) and the golden urn, containing manna, Hebrews ix. Hence the pagans perhaps took occasion to keep their secret mysteries in an ark, cista secretorum. (Apuleius, Met. ) (Calmet) The ark was three feet nine inches long, two feet three inches high, and as much in breadth.
